run_kind_note: First 5 instances of the validation split per subtask (15 LLM calls total). Pipeline reproducibility check, mirror-scope of the 02b87497_2026-04-29T14-58Z run.
purpose: Reproducibility / cross-run comparison against the 2026-04-29T14-58Z snapshot, executed against the same upstream commit (02b87497) with the latest `tasks/validator.py` actually run end-to-end on each artifact (rather than skipped).
model_note: Cursor-subagent invocation; model slug is the one accepted by the Task tool. Closest direct mapping is Anthropic claude-sonnet-4 (i.e., the Sonnet 4 family).
runner_note: 15 fresh Cursor subagents (subagent_type=generalPurpose, model=claude-4-sonnet, one per instance) launched in a single parallel batch. Same 15 prompt files as the 2026-04-29T14-58Z run (byte-identical, copied across), responses captured fresh.

paper.title: AbsenceBench: Language Models Can't Tell What's Missing

validation.shacl_validator_outcome: FAIL on all 4 of our JSON-LDs against the current upstream shapes file. Failures are due to two known shape bugs (Bug A: TaskProblemShape PropertyShape malformed; Bug B: EvaluationTaskShape qualifiedValueShape missing). See ../../../pdf2ct/shacl_run.txt for the captured pyshacl output and ../../../pdf2ct/validation_report.json for structured per-bug attribution. Both bugs match items on the team's known-issue list (RISEBench agent analysis, Slack 2026-04-29: Changes 2 and 4).
validation.shacl_validator_testdata_state: Leo's testdata is partially passing on 02b87497: valid_solution.jsonld + direct_task.jsonld + valid_solution_subtasks_all_concrete.jsonld PASS; valid_problem.jsonld + valid_evaluation_task.jsonld FAIL (same Bugs A and B respectively). So the bugs are general, not specific to our files.

comparison_to_previous_run.scope_match: Identical scope: same parent commit (02b87497), same prompts (byte-identical), same model (claude-4-sonnet via Cursor subagent), same hyperparameters (T=0, max_tokens=4096), same n=5/subset.

aggregate_metric_deltas.note: Δ = new − old. Recall went up across the board, precision dropped slightly (model was chattier this run on poetry). Numerical fully deterministic.

per_instance_response_identity.note: Whether the LLM's literal raw_response is byte-identical between the two runs. Cursor subagents at 'T=0' are NOT strictly deterministic for non-trivial outputs.

comparison_to_paper_table_3.note: Paper Table 3 reports micro-F1 across the FULL validation split; ours is on n=5 per subset. Comparison shown for orientation only; the n=5 sample is too small to support claims about model improvement vs. claude-3-7-sonnet.

command_line: Cursor-subagent path: 15 Task() invocations of subagent_type=generalPurpose with model=claude-4-sonnet (see runner_note); subsequently a small inline ingest python snippet that mirrors absencebench_implementation.py:run_subset / aggregate / update_solution_file. Equivalent direct-API command for a fresh run on a different machine: `python absencebench_implementation.py --dry-run --model claude-sonnet-4-5` (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).

agent_transcript.note: Lives on the contributor's machine; session-scoped Cursor metadata; not portable. Referenced for audit but not part of this commit. The implementation script + this manifest + the per-instance outputs_*.jsonl + committed prompts/ dir are sufficient for full reproducibility (modulo Cursor-subagent run-to-run nondeterminism documented in comparison_to_previous_run.per_instance_response_identity).
